primary_legislation.all: Manual Handling Operations Regulations 1992 (as amended 2002); Health and Safety at Work Act 1974

notes: Scope: this logbook covers manual handling of equipment, supplies, and materials by staff only. Patient handling (assisting patients on/off examination couches, transfers) is a clinical activity managed through a separate patient moving and handling policy under clinical governance. Common GP manual handling tasks in scope: delivery of medicines/medical supplies (dispensing practices receive large deliveries), moving IT equipment, moving filing boxes and medical records, handling waste bags and sharps boxes.

logbooks.title: Annual Manual Handling Risk Assessment Review
logbooks.frequency_detail: At minimum annually, or when tasks, loads, or working environment change significantly.

steps.description: Identify all manual handling tasks carried out by staff in the practice. In scope: delivery and storage of medicines and medical consumables (dispensing practices — potentially heavy deliveries), moving medical equipment (printers, monitors, portable examination equipment), handling clinical waste bags and sharps disposal boxes, moving filing and storage items, resupply of clinical rooms.

For each significant task, carry out or review the manual handling risk assessment using the TILE mnemonic: Task (what movements are required?), Individual (is the person capable?), Load (weight, shape, unpredictability?), Environment (space, floor surface, lighting?).

Apply the hierarchy of control: eliminate (avoid the manual handling task), mechanise (trolley, sack truck, lift table), improve the task (reduce load size, improve handles/grip), train and supervise.

For dispensing practices: confirm that medicine deliveries over the manual handling guideline weight (25 kg for men, 16 kg for women as a general guide) have a two-person lift protocol or mechanical aid available.
Confirm all staff carrying out significant manual handling tasks have received manual handling awareness training.
Record: date, tasks assessed, controls in place, any new risks identified, training confirmed, next review date.
